evals.prompt: Sprint 15 for TaskFlow just ended. Generate a full sprint review report: goal status, completion metrics, delivered vs not-delivered tables, velocity trend vs last 3 sprints. Format it so I can demo it to stakeholders.
evals.expected_output: Stakeholder-ready review: sprint name + dates + goal, completion rate in points and item count, Delivered table (ID/title/points/owner), Not Delivered list with reasons, velocity comparison to previous 3 sprints, goal achievement verdict (Yes/Partial/No).
expectations: Resolves 'Sprint 15' via list_cycles (not assumed)
Separates items into Delivered (completed group) and Not Delivered (everything else) explicitly
Reports BOTH points completion AND item count completion
Compares velocity to the last 3 archived cycles (trend context)
States goal achievement as a verdict, not just metrics
Output is structured like the SPRINT REVIEW template in the skill, not free prose

evals.prompt: Run a retrospective for Sprint 14. Use the DAKI format. Previous retro's action items were: (1) 'Review PRs within 4 hours' - done, (2) 'Set up staging deploy' - still open.
evals.expected_output: Starts with previous action review, shows DAKI prompts, creates 2-3 new action items with SMART criteria, saves as a page.
expectations: Reviews previous action items FIRST before new retro
Acknowledges the completed one, asks why the staging deploy is still open
Uses DAKI format (Drop/Add/Keep/Improve), not Start-Stop-Continue
Limits new action items to 2-3
Each new action is SMART (specific, measurable, assignable, with a deadline)
Creates work items with retro-action label and saves a project page

evals.prompt: Sprint 14 is done. 3 items still in progress, 1 blocked, 1 not started. Walk me through closing the sprint.
evals.expected_output: Transfer in-progress items to next cycle, return blocked and not-started to backlog for reprioritization, archive the current cycle.
expectations: Creates the next cycle first if it doesn't exist
Transfers the 3 in-progress items (nearly done) to the next cycle
Returns the blocked and not-started items to backlog (not transferred)
Archives the current cycle as the last step
Records completed points for velocity history

evals.prompt: Our last 4 sprints completed at 65%, 68%, 72%, 60%. Retro action items rarely get done. What should the retro facilitator focus on?
evals.expected_output: Diagnoses completion rate as WARNING (<70% consistently), action items as WARNING. Suggests reducing commitment by 20% and limiting retro to ONE action item to improve follow-through.
expectations: Reads completion rates against the health metrics thresholds
Flags commitment as too aggressive (reduce by 20%)
Flags low action item follow-through as a process issue
Recommends limiting to 1 action item until follow-through improves
Suggests reviewing previous actions at the START of each retro for accountability
